content:
- Updated the task file to status done and passes=true, with acceptance boxes checked and gate evidence recorded.
- Ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully; it updated Ralph bookkeeping and sent the finished-task email.
- Final step is a full git add/commit/push containing README, task metadata, progress log, and generated Ralph bookkeeping/evidence files.

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` after loading the prior progress log.
- Active candidates were one failing low-priority project-wide code hygiene task and one recurring meta-task; there were no active failing bugs and no blocker tags on the selected task.
- Per precedence rules, chose `.ralph/tasks/story-project-wide-code-hygiene/01-task-audit-and-replace-magic-numbers-project-wide.md` because any unblocked `<passes>false</passes>` task outranks meta-task, and it is the only such active task. Next step is to write that exact path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` and quit immediately.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and found active task .ralph/tasks/story-project-wide-code-hygiene/01-task-audit-and-replace-magic-numbers-project-wide.md.
- Read prior progress entry; previous turn had already marked a different task finished in Ralph bookkeeping.
- Reading the active task file now to determine whether this turn should plan, verify, or execute.

- Spawned-agent audit focused on src/ha, src/process, and worker-related code for project-wide magic-number cleanup planning.
- Main runtime hotspots are process worker output draining constants (8192, 1ms, 256*1024), pg_ctl default wait_seconds 30, and HA lower step-count integers that should become named constants or typed step counts.
- Most numbered values in HA worker/process tests are fixture data or assertion sentinels, not worth productizing; recommend shared test constants only for repeated timing/version scaffolding.

- Determined active task is still in planning state; no TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE marker exists yet.
- Explored high-density numeric-literal hotspots with ripgrep and started reading key files: src/process/worker.rs, src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs, src/api/worker.rs, src/config/parser.rs, src/test_harness/ports.rs, src/runtime/node.rs.
- Spawned three explorers to audit runtime code, test/harness code, and HA/process slices in parallel so the plan can name concrete targets and non-targets.

- Audited runtime `src/` numerics with grep plus direct reads of process worker, api worker, debug api worker, config parser/defaults/schema, and bin startup.
- Main worthwhile cleanup targets are process worker output-drain limits and poll timeouts, API accept/read/TLS timeouts plus request-id truncation, debug UI polling interval, schema default `protect_recent_seconds = 300`, and CLI runtime worker thread count / exit codes.
- Values already handled well should mostly stay inline when they are protocol/domain obvious: HTTP status codes, TLS ClientHello first byte 0x16, single-byte peek buffer, sequence increment of 1, quote-capacity +2, CR/LF/NUL checks.

- Audited the active magic-number cleanup task file; it is still at the planning phase with no plan/marker yet.
- Explored repo hotspots and sampled runtime, HA/process, logging, harness, and e2e support files to separate true magic numbers from obvious fixtures.
- Next step is to patch the task file with a detailed phase-by-phase plan ending in TO BE VERIFIED, then stop immediately as instructed.

- Wrote a detailed execution plan into .ralph/tasks/story-project-wide-code-hygiene/01-task-audit-and-replace-magic-numbers-project-wide.md.
- The plan now includes hotspot findings, classification rules, runtime/harness/test cleanup phases, likely files, execution order, and skeptical-review targets.
- Stopped with the terminal marker set to TO BE VERIFIED exactly as requested; next turn must do a deep skeptical review, alter at least one concrete part of the plan, and only then promote it.

- Runtime and HA/process audits converged on the real execution targets: process worker drain constants, API worker timeouts/request-id limits, HA lower step-count literals, and selected shared test constants.
- Test/harness review confirmed many numeric literals are domain fixtures (ports, status codes, versions, seed timestamps) and should stay inline; the plan should explicitly protect against bogus configurability.
- Writing the task plan now and stopping at TO BE VERIFIED per instruction.

- Confirmed the active task file now contains a detailed execution plan plus a skeptical-review section and ends with TO BE VERIFIED.
- Stopped at the required planning boundary: no implementation patches, no gate runs, no docs edits, and no task-switch/commit/push actions in this turn.
- Next turn should read the same task file, perform the required deep skeptical review, alter at least one concrete part of the plan, and only then replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.

- Skeptical-review pass on the magic-number cleanup plan found a concrete gap: src/api/worker.rs has production timing/request-size literals that the plan had only treated as test cleanup.
- Focused repo scan also confirmed src/test_harness/runtime_config.rs is a central owner for canonical fixture defaults, so the docs-update rule should mention contributor-facing sample config drift more explicitly.
- Next step is to patch the task file by changing the runtime scope and execution order, alter at least one concrete plan item, replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE, and then stop immediately.

- Completed the required TO BE VERIFIED phase for the active magic-number cleanup task and altered the plan in concrete ways before promoting it.
- Task plan now explicitly includes src/api/worker.rs runtime constants, src/config/defaults.rs ownership checks, a stricter execution order, and a broader docs-update trigger for canonical fixture defaults.
- Stopping immediately with the task file ending in NOW EXECUTE; the next turn should execute the written plan without restarting broad exploration.

- Read startup progress log and .ralph/current_task.txt; active task is .ralph/tasks/story-project-wide-code-hygiene/01-task-audit-and-replace-magic-numbers-project-wide.md.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether this turn is in planning, skeptical review, or execution mode, because the user workflow requires stopping at the phase boundary.
- If the file still ends in TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE, I must follow that phase exactly and avoid mixing stages.

- Active task file already ends in NOW EXECUTE, so I have switched from planning into implementation and must not restart broad exploration.
- Starting with the plan's first required ownership decision: inspect src/api/worker.rs and src/config/defaults.rs to decide which values stay as internal constants versus existing config defaults.
- I will then delegate the remaining runtime, harness, and test/support slices in parallel once those owners are fixed.

- Confirmed src/config/defaults.rs already owns explicit operator-facing defaults; next check is whether src/api/worker.rs literals are internal protocol/IO constants or should join existing config surface.
- Reading the specific request parsing and timeout sections now so I can patch concrete names instead of guessing.
- After that, I can safely split the remaining cleanup into runtime, harness, and HA/test support workers with disjoint ownership.

- First api/worker patch attempt failed on one test-helper context mismatch, so I am re-reading only the exact touched spans and reapplying a narrower edit.
- No plan change: still naming internal API timeouts and request-parsing limits at module scope and reusing them in local test helpers.
- After this lands, I can delegate the remaining disjoint runtime/harness/test-support slices.

- Landed the first main-agent runtime ownership patch in src/api/worker.rs, naming internal API poll/timeout/request-limit constants and reusing them in local response-framing helpers.
- Spawned three disjoint workers for runtime, harness/examples, and HA/test-support cleanup so the remaining slices can proceed in parallel without file overlap.
- Preparing integration now by checking Makefile gate targets and docs layout, and I am running a targeted cargo test for the api worker slice while waiting for worker results.

- API worker constants are now named at module scope; continuing the NOW EXECUTE pass directly without subagents because that tool path is unavailable here.
- Running a focused numeric audit over the remaining runtime hotspot files from the task plan: process worker, postgres ingest, HA worker/dispatch, DCS worker, and runtime node.
- Goal is the same: name repeated internal thresholds and waits, leave obvious domain fixtures inline, and avoid inventing new config surface.

- Worker slice narrowed to harness/builders/examples. Reading only the owned hotspot sections before patching.
- The likely owners are shared canonical fixture defaults in src/test_harness/runtime_config.rs plus small module-level constants for retry budgets and poll sleeps in ports/util/etcd3.
- I will keep intentional topology-specific ports inline and avoid turning harness internals into product config.

- HA/test-support slice audit shows the main cleanup value is semantic timing constants in multi_node.rs and partition.rs plus a smaller shared-wait cleanup in worker_contract_tests.rs.
- I am reading only the repeated timeout/polling sections and avoiding broad test rewrites, because many remaining literals are obvious status-code or fixture-domain values.
- Next step is to add module-level semantic constants and swap repeated sleep/timeout calls to those names.

- Focused audit narrowed the runtime changes to concrete owners: process output drain chunk/limit constants, runtime startup drain/poll constants, and postgres ingest per-file caps.
- Reading only the exact spans around those repeats now to avoid broad wandering during NOW EXECUTE.
- I will patch runtime module constants first, then sweep the repeated test-only waits in the same files if they materially improve readability.

- The targeted cargo test for the API slice is compiling; I am polling it while I also wait for the three parallel workers to finish their disjoint cleanup scopes.
- Makefile confirms the required final gates are make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint; docs are mdBook-backed with docs/src/SUMMARY.md as the visible source index.
- Once workers return, I will review and integrate their patches, then run the full gate sequence and update the task bookkeeping only after everything passes.

- I have enough concrete context to patch the runtime-owned constants directly and then fold the most repetitive test-only waits into local test constants in the same files.
- Next edits will target process worker drain chunk/byte budgets, runtime startup drain/poll constants, postgres ingest file-size caps, and repeated 5ms/10ms/50ms test waits in HA and DCS workers.
- I am avoiding the scenario-fixture IP/port literals because those are topology data, not real magic-number cleanup targets.

- Targeted compile/test on src/api/worker.rs exposed a nested-test visibility issue for the new module constants; fixing that now is a small local integration step, not a design change.
- Worker branches are still running; I am polling them in parallel so I can review their edits as soon as they return.
- No docs changes yet; I only need docs updates if canonical defaults or contributor-facing ownership guidance actually moved.

- The api/worker visibility fix is in and the targeted cargo test is rerunning.
- I am also polling the three worker branches and reviewing my own src/api/worker.rs diff so integration stays controlled and bounded by the task plan.
- Next checkpoint is the first completed worker result; after that I will inspect repo diffs, format if needed, and move toward the full make gates.

- The targeted api::worker test run passed after importing the new shared constants into the nested test module.
- Main thread is now blocked only on the three worker slices returning; after that I will review the merged worktree diffs, format, and run the full required make gates.
- Current visible local change is src/api/worker.rs plus Ralph bookkeeping files from the task workflow.

- Shared worktree now shows worker edits in harness and HA/test-support files: src/test_harness/ports.rs, src/test_harness/runtime_config.rs, src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs, src/worker_contract_tests.rs, and tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs.
- Reviewing the actual diffs now to verify they followed the task classification rules and did not invent config knobs or over-abstract scenario readability.
- Also scanning docs/source references for canonical fixture defaults in case any of the landed harness constants require a contributor-facing docs adjustment.

- Harness/builders/examples slice patched: named canonical sample defaults in runtime_config, extracted harness retry/wait constants in ports/util/etcd3, and named the smoke example listen address/poll interval.
- Running formatting and a compile-oriented verification pass now to catch any local integration mistakes before reporting back to the parent agent.
- If compile is clean, I will summarize the changed files and note that docs should not need updates because only constant ownership changed, not fixture values.

- Harness/builders/examples worker finished local edits in runtime_config, ports, ha_e2e/startup, ha_e2e/util, etcd3, and the debug_ui_smoke_server example.
- Shared canonical sample defaults are now named in runtime_config; harness retry budgets and wait durations are named in ports/util/etcd3; startup.rs reuses named HA/process/logging fixture defaults across both JSON payload generation and RuntimeConfigBuilder assembly.
- cargo fmt succeeded. cargo check --all-targets no longer reports issues in my slice; remaining failures are in other workers' files (src/process/worker.rs type mismatch earlier, then src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs missing REAL_INGEST_RETRY_SLEEP references), so parent should re-run after those land.

- Reviewed the first landed worker diffs; they align with the plan by introducing semantic constants in harness and HA support code without creating new operator config.
- Still waiting on completion of the remaining runtime and partition-support work, which has not fully surfaced in git status yet.
- Once all worker slices settle, the next steps are cargo fmt, full diff review, then make check/test/test-long/lint in that order.

- Landed the HA/test-support cleanup in progress: added semantic timing constants to multi_node.rs and partition.rs and shared test wait constants to worker_contract_tests.rs.
- The cleanup is intentionally narrow: poll cadences, fallback caps, workload sample intervals, and repeated test waits; I left obvious scenario-specific durations and fixture-domain literals inline.
- Running formatting and a targeted test/check pass now to catch any compile issues before reporting back to the main agent.

- Runtime and partition-support diffs have now surfaced as well, so the integrated worktree appears close to complete.
- Polling worker completion one more time while running cargo fmt across the workspace, which is the last integration step before the required full make gates.
- After formatting I will review any notable remaining diffs and then start make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint in order.

- Harness/examples worker completed and reported two remaining integration issues from other slices: a u32/u64 mismatch in src/process/worker.rs and missing REAL_INGEST_RETRY_SLEEP references in src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs.
- I am running cargo check now to reproduce the exact compiler output and will fix those directly in the main thread rather than waiting for another round-trip.
- Remaining workers for runtime and HA/test-support are still running; I will merge their final statuses after the compile is green again.

- Targeted cargo test --no-run for my slice is blocked by unrelated concurrent compile failures outside my owned files: src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs references missing REAL_INGEST_RETRY_SLEEP and src/process/worker.rs has a PG_CTL_DEFAULT_WAIT_SECONDS type mismatch.
- I did not patch those unrelated files from this worker. My owned files are formatted and locally reviewed via diff.
- Handoff should mention both external blockers plus the exact files I changed in this slice.

- cargo check currently fails only in src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs because the new REAL_INGEST_RETRY_SLEEP constant is not visible from the specific nested test code that uses it.
- Reading that file's test-module structure now so I can fix the scope cleanly, then I will rerun cargo check to expose the next compile issue if any.
- The remaining worker results can be integrated after compile is restored; right now the main blocker is this local build error.

- Landed the main runtime magic-number cleanup in api/process/runtime/logging plus local test constants in dcs and ha worker test modules.
- Remaining audit in those same files now looks mostly like named constants or intentional one-off test values; I am running formatting and a final grep before targeted verification.
- Next is compile/test feedback, then I still need to cover harness and HA support files before the full make gates and Ralph closeout.

- Reproduced the remaining compile errors exactly: REAL_INGEST_RETRY_SLEEP is referenced from nested real_binary tests without visibility, and PG_CTL_DEFAULT_WAIT_SECONDS has the wrong integer width for spec.wait_seconds.
- Fixing both directly now in the main thread so the integrated workspace can compile before waiting further on agent notifications.
- After these patches I will rerun cargo check and then move into the full make gates once the worker statuses are settled.

- Runtime-slice cleanup landed. I am now in the harness/builders/examples phase from the task plan, auditing only the named files for repeated fixture defaults and support timing constants.
- I expect central ownership in src/test_harness/runtime_config.rs for canonical sample addresses/ports/default intervals, and local constants in ports/startup/net_proxy/examples for retry sleeps and budgets.
- After harness cleanup I still need the HA scenario-support sweep and the full make gates.

- Patched the two concrete integration blockers directly: imported the ingest retry/test constants into logging/postgres_ingest.rs::tests::real_binary and changed PG_CTL_DEFAULT_WAIT_SECONDS to u64 in src/process/worker.rs.
- Rerunning cargo check --all-targets now to confirm the integrated worktree compiles cleanly before the required make gates.
- Still waiting on the final runtime-slice worker notification, but its visible edits are already in the worktree.

- cargo check is rerunning after the logging/process integration fixes; once it clears, there is no more pre-gate work left besides optionally reviewing the last worker summary.
- I am also capturing the consolidated diff for the touched implementation files so I can sanity-check ownership and docs impact before the full make gate sequence.
- If the last runtime worker does not reply soon, I can still proceed because its edits are already visible in the shared worktree and under main-thread review.

- Harness slice needed only small follow-up patches because most canonical defaults were already named; now moving to the HA support/test cleanup phase.
- I am auditing tests/ha/support/* plus the remaining test modules for repeated scenario polling and timeout windows, aiming for semantic constants without turning the scenarios into unreadable constant soup.
- After this pass I should be ready for targeted checks and then the required make gates.

- cargo check exposed one warning that would fail later lint gates: SAMPLE_DCS_INIT_PAYLOAD_JSON was test-only but compiled in normal builds. I gated it with #[cfg(test)].
- Rerunning cargo check --all-targets now to confirm the integrated worktree is warning-free before the required make gate sequence.
- Still waiting on the final runtime worker status, but there is no remaining blocker to proceed once compile is clean.

- Final pre-gate cargo check is running after the #[cfg(test)] cleanup for the DCS init payload constant.
- If it returns clean, the next commands are the full required make gates in order; if not, I will fix whatever remaining warning/error appears before starting those gates.
- The visible worktree still contains only the planned magic-number cleanup files plus Ralph bookkeeping.

- cargo check found another nested-test visibility issue, this time in src/test_harness/ports.rs for PORT_ALLOCATION_SETTLE_POLL_INTERVAL, plus the resulting unused import/dead-code warning.
- Reading the exact test module structure now so I can scope or import that constant correctly and keep the workspace warning-free.
- After this patch I will rerun cargo check again; the pattern suggests the remaining blockers are local integration details rather than design issues.

- Fixed the ports.rs nested-test import issue and removed the now-unused Duration import from that test module.
- Rerunning cargo check --all-targets yet again; once it is clean there should be no remaining pre-gate integration work.
- After that I still need to inspect the runtime worker's touched files (src/dcs/worker.rs and src/ha/worker.rs) as part of final diff sanity-checking.

- cargo check is in flight again after the ports.rs import fix.
- In parallel I am reviewing the remaining runtime-worker changes in src/dcs/worker.rs and src/ha/worker.rs so there are no surprises left before the full make gates.
- The repeated pattern so far has been nested test modules needing explicit imports for newly named constants; runtime behavior itself still looks consistent with the plan.

- Gated PORT_ALLOCATION_SETTLE_POLL_INTERVAL with #[cfg(test)] because it is only used by the nested tests and would otherwise fail the later warning-as-error lint gates.
- Running one final cargo check --all-targets now; if it is clean, I will switch directly to make check and continue through the full required gate list.
- The runtime-worker changes in src/dcs/worker.rs and src/ha/worker.rs look straightforward and consistent with the task rules.

- Final cargo check preflight is being polled now after the last #[cfg(test)] cleanup.
- Once it clears, there is no more scoped integration work left; I can move to make check, then make test, then make test-long, then make lint exactly as the task requires.
- I will only touch docs/task bookkeeping after those full gates pass.

- Completed the planned code slices: runtime owners, harness defaults, and the HA support scenario windows now have additional named constants where repetition was real.
- Entering verification now; next actions are formatting plus the full required make gates, with task-file pass bookkeeping deferred until all gates are clean.
- If any gate fails, I will treat that as part of the task and keep iterating instead of declaring partial completion.

- cargo check preflight is clean and the required gate sequence has started with make check.
- I will stay on the gate path now: pass/fix/re-run each gate in order without switching context.
- Remaining runtime worker notification is non-blocking at this point because the worktree already contains and compiles the visible runtime-slice edits.

- make check completed cleanly and I have advanced to make test.
- cargo briefly blocked on the package cache during make test, likely due to another compile process, but it then started compiling normally.
- I am polling the test gate now and will only deviate if a real test failure appears.

- make test remains in progress with no surfaced failure output yet; I am continuing to poll it rather than speculating.
- If it stalls, I will inspect the running process state, but right now the correct move is to stay on the gate until it exits.
- The remaining background worker notification still has not arrived, but it is no longer on the critical path.

- make test is in the middle of the full nextest run and is passing so far across the updated runtime, harness, and HA support areas.
- I will hold the gate until it exits cleanly, then immediately advance to make test-long.
- Any remaining runtime-worker notification is now purely informational.

- make test passed cleanly: 428/428 tests passed with 9 skipped by profile.
- The required make test-long gate is now running, starting with the ultra-long nextest profile before Docker compose validation and both smoke scripts.
- I will stay on this gate until the entire target exits, because this is one of the hard completion requirements in the task file.

- make test-long is still in progress with no surfaced failure output yet.
- I am polling both the gate output and the underlying long-running commands because this target includes ultra-long nextest execution plus Docker validation and smoke coverage.
- No changes are being made during this phase; the correct move is to hold the gate and inspect any failure only if one appears.

- make test-long has reported progress in the ultra-long nextest phase, with the first long HA scenarios passing.
- After the 9-test nextest profile completes, this same gate will still run compose-config-check.sh, smoke-single.sh, and smoke-cluster.sh.
- I am not doing side work during this phase so the gate output stays the critical path.

- make test-long remains in progress; no failure has surfaced, and the long-ha scenarios are the expected time sink here.
- I am polling both the gate stream and the process table to distinguish the ultra-long nextest phase from the later Docker validation/smoke phase.
- Once this target exits cleanly, only make lint and the final Ralph/git bookkeeping remain.

- Ultra-long gate progress continues: the third of nine long scenarios has passed.
- I am holding the same poll loop until make test-long finishes, because any premature switching would only slow down the required gate sequence.
- No new code changes have been made since entering the gate phase.

- make test-long has reached 4/9 passing ultra-long scenarios and still shows no failure output.
- I am polling both the gate stream and process table to watch for the later compose/smoke phases once the nextest phase ends.
- The gate path remains the only critical path item right now.

- make test-long has reached 5/9 passing ultra-long scenarios with no regressions surfaced.
- I am continuing to hold the gate until both the long nextest portion and the later Docker validation/smoke steps finish.
- Once this target exits cleanly, the remaining required gate is make lint.

- make test-long is now 6/9 through the ultra-long nextest phase.
- I am watching for the handoff into the Docker compose/smoke substeps that happen after those nine tests complete.
- No failures or warnings have surfaced during the gate sequence so far.

- make test-long finished the ultra-long nextest section and has moved into the Docker smoke coverage phase.
- smoke-single.sh is currently bringing up the single-node compose stack; smoke-cluster.sh will follow before the gate exits.
- I am staying on the same gate until both Docker smoke phases complete, because test-long only passes when the whole target finishes.

- Another ultra-long test result arrived during the test-long transition; I am still treating the entire make test-long target as one indivisible gate.
- Watching both stdout and the Docker smoke processes now so I can tell when single-node coverage hands off to cluster coverage and when the full target finally exits.
- No action needed unless the gate emits a concrete failure.

- Ultra-long nextest is fully green: 9/9 passed and compose-config-check passed.
- make test-long is now in smoke-single.sh, currently building and starting the single-node Docker image/stack before it will move to cluster smoke.
- I am monitoring both the gate stream and docker ps so I can tell whether the stack is coming up cleanly.

- smoke-single is still in the Docker image build phase, compiling the release binary inside the builder image.
- No gate failure has surfaced; I am continuing to hold make test-long and watch for the container startup/health-check phase after the image build completes.
- Cluster smoke will still follow after single-node smoke succeeds.

- smoke-single is near the end of the in-container Rust release build and should transition into container startup next.
- I am polling for docker ps output and gate logs so I can catch readiness/health-check failures immediately if they happen.
- Nothing to fix yet; still following the required gate path.

- smoke-single finished the image build and has moved into compose startup plus container health checks.
- I am watching docker ps and the gate output for the etcd/node health transitions and any readiness failures.
- There appear to be two smoke-single projects in docker ps because of overlapping gate wrappers, but the active test-long gate is still the one being polled from session 92232.

- make test-long has passed the single-node smoke step and moved into cluster smoke.
- The cluster compose stack is starting now, currently waiting on etcd health before the three node containers can settle.
- I will keep polling until the full test-long target exits, because only then can I move to make lint.

- make test-long completed cleanly, including 9/9 ultra-long tests plus compose-config-check, smoke-single, and smoke-cluster.
- The final required gate make lint is running and has already cleared docs lint plus the no-silent-errors guard; it is now in cargo clippy.
- I will only touch task bookkeeping and git/Ralph completion steps after this final gate is green.

- All required gates are green: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint passed after the project-wide magic-number cleanup.
- Updated the active task file to status done with passes=true and checked acceptance boxes.
- Running .ralph/task_switch.sh now, then I need a final git add/commit/push with the task summary and gate evidence included.
